zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Holt於1670年4月26日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利時，她的父親Major Robert Holt II16歲，母親Joanne Ligon17歲。她於1695年與John Hancock在美國維吉尼亞州素利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1734年5月21日在她的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在美國維吉尼亞州素利。

nameMeanings.name: Holt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, Danish, and Norwegian: topographic name for someone who lived in or by a small wood, from Middle English, Middle Low German, Danish, and Norwegian holt, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word. In England, the surname is widely distributed, but more common in Lancashire and Yorkshire than elsewhere.

variants.name: Hohlt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Ordell.
North German: variant of Holt with the additional h reflecting the lengthened vowel or a variant of Hohl with the addition of excrescent -t.
Scandinavian: variant of   Holt  .

variants.name: Holten
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hjalmer, Lars, Nils.

Dutch (also Van Holten) and North German: habitational name from any of the places so called, from Low German holt ‘holt, copse, small wood’. There are two in the Dutch province of Overijssel and another near Oberhausen in the Rhineland.
Danish: variant of   Holt  .

variants.name: Holtan
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads so named, from the definite plural form of holt ‘small wood’ (see Holt ).

variants.name: Holter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Selmer, Hilmar, Knute, Lars.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads in southeastern Norway, from the indefinite plural of holt ‘holt, small wood’ (see Holt ).
North German (also Hölter): habitational name for someone from any of several places in Westphalia called Hölt and Holt (see Holt ). Compare Hoelter .

variants.name: Holtman
meanings: Dutch: variant of Holt + man ‘man’.
Americanized form of German   Holtmann  .

variants.name: Holtmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Udo, Aloys, Hans, Heinz.
North German: variant of   Holt  .

variants.name: Holte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Bjorg, Erik, Helmer, Hjalmer, Knute, Nels, Nordahl, Selmer, Swen, Thorvald.
North German: variant of   Holt  .
North German: habitational name from any of several places so named.

variants.name: Hoult
meanings: English: variant of   Holt  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Holt was born on 26 April 1670, in Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Major Robert Holt II, was 16 and her mother, Joanne Ligon, was 17. She married John Hancock in 1695, in Surry, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 21 May 1734, in her hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Surry, Virginia, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Holt est née le 26 avril 1670, à Surry, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Major Robert Holt II, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Joanne Ligon, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé John Hancock en 1695, à Surry, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 mai 1734, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Surry, Virginie, États-Unis.

Sie hat 1695 in Surry, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten John Hancock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 21. Mai 1734 im Alter von 64 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Surry, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Holt(이)가 1670년 4월 26일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Major Robert Holt II(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanne Ligon(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1695년 미국 버지니아 주 수리에서 John Hancock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1734년 5월 21일 그녀의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 버지니아 주 수리에 묻혔다.

ru.Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial: сурри, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Holt родилась 26 апреля 1670 г. в местности сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Major Robert Holt II было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Joanne Ligon, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hancock в 1695 г. в местности сурри, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 21 мая 1734 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности сурри, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты.

Se casó con John Hancock en 1695, en Surry, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 21 de mayo de 1734, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Surry, Virginia, Estados Unidos.
